clusterWarnings: Source-swap negation repeats in Sylvanas's inference beats.

findings.line: That reached him. Not far, but enough to make him feel it and refuse it.
findings.deletionTest: That reached him enough to make him feel it and refuse it.
findings.reason: The negative half is scaffolding; the sentence survives once the contrast is removed.
findings.suggestedFix: That reached him far enough to make him feel it and refuse it.

findings.line: Not imagination now.
findings.reason: The fragment only rejects a possibility before the next sentence supplies the real claim.
findings.suggestedFix: Real now.

findings.line: No tremor now. Worse.
findings.reason: The rejected state is dead-weight setup for the actual judgment.
findings.suggestedFix: Worse now.

findings.line: He hesitated. Not from doubt. From knowing exactly what answer was wanted and disliking how near it stood to the truth.
findings.deletionTest: He hesitated from knowing exactly what answer was wanted and disliking how near it stood to the truth.
findings.reason: The construction rejects one cause before naming the real one, so the first half is expendable scaffold.
findings.suggestedFix: He hesitated from knowing exactly which answer they wanted and disliking how near it stood to the truth.

findings.line: Not because he lacked authority. Because the one he needed had not been given.
findings.deletionTest: Because the one he needed had not been given.
findings.reason: The sentence reaches its real cause only after a negation feint.
findings.suggestedFix: Because the authority he needed had not been given.

clusterFlags: Tribunal sections repeatedly give the room or chamber human agency.
Analytical POV compression is laundering judgment onto abstractions.

findings.line: Above them, the blade sat in a box and still managed to teach the palace its name.
findings.subject: blade
findings.realAgent: its spreading cold, the guards' reactions, and the visible effects moving through the palace
findings.reason: The blade is active, but the sentence still launders observable spread into a pedagogical verb instead of naming the effects.
findings.suggestedFix: Above them, the blade sat in a box, and its cold had already worked through the palace.

findings.pattern: P5 objects wanting, refusing, or deciding
findings.line: It depended on whether the room wanted truth before it arrived.
findings.subject: room
findings.realAgent: the magistrates, clerk, and guards deciding whether to hear the truth
findings.reason: The desire belongs to the people present, not to the room itself.
findings.suggestedFix: It depended on whether anyone in the room wanted truth before it arrived.

findings.line: Even Stratholme served her.
findings.subject: Stratholme
findings.realAgent: Ashcombe, who is folding Stratholme into her prosecution chain
findings.reason: The city is not doing the argumentative work; Ashcombe is.
findings.suggestedFix: Ashcombe turned even Stratholme to her use.

findings.line: Then the lid came up and the room found the edge of its wrongness.
findings.subject: room
findings.realAgent: the people in the chamber feeling the blade's wrongness when the lid opens
findings.reason: Perception belongs to the witnesses, not to the room.
findings.suggestedFix: Then the lid came up and everyone in the room felt the edge of its wrongness.

findings.line: By the time Uther was called, the chamber was ready to believe anything that left blame outside itself.
findings.subject: chamber
findings.realAgent: the people in the chamber, already primed by fear and prosecution framing
findings.reason: Belief belongs to the audience, not to the architecture.
findings.suggestedFix: By the time Uther was called, the people in the chamber were ready to believe anything that left blame elsewhere.

findings.line: When he rose to object, the whole room shifted toward him.
findings.subject: room
findings.realAgent: the people in the hall turning toward Antonidas
findings.reason: The physical movement belongs to the watchers, not to the room.
findings.suggestedFix: When he rose to object, every head in the chamber turned toward him.

findings.line: The room listened to the state, not the sister.
findings.subject: room
findings.realAgent: the listeners in the hall choosing official authority over Vereesa's familial presence
findings.reason: Listening is being laundered onto the room instead of the crowd.
findings.suggestedFix: The listeners heard the state, not the sister.

findings.line: The stretch a court order might imagine while men wrote letters and filed seals had already burned away.
findings.subject: court order
findings.realAgent: men in office imagining they still had time for procedure
findings.reason: The cognitive act belongs to officeholders, not to the order itself.
findings.suggestedFix: Whatever time men with seals might imagine while they wrote letters and filed orders had already burned away.

findings.line: A plan tried to form under her ribs.
findings.subject: plan
findings.realAgent: Sylvanas beginning to piece a plan together
findings.reason: The mental act belongs to Sylvanas, not to the plan as an external agent.
findings.suggestedFix: She began piecing together a plan under her ribs.

findings.pattern: P5 objects wanting, refusing, or deciding
findings.line: If there is a room left willing to hear.
findings.subject: room
findings.realAgent: any remaining people still willing to hear testimony
findings.reason: Willingness is being assigned to the room instead of to people.
findings.suggestedFix: If there is anyone left willing to hear.

findings.line: That got his eyes up.
findings.reason: This is a generic reaction beat and one of several eye-led response cues in the chapter.
findings.suggestedFix: That pulled his attention up.

findings.line: Sylvanas looked to Jaina despite herself.
findings.reason: This is a generic connection beat, weaker than the more specific eye/gaze choices later in the chapter.
findings.suggestedFix: Sylvanas turned to Jaina despite herself.

findings.line: Jaina finally looked at her.
findings.reason: This is another generic look beat and is weaker than the later, more specific strain markers on Jaina.
findings.suggestedFix: Jaina finally turned to her.

findings.line: Thornton's eyes lifted from the papers more than once.
findings.reason: This repeats the same reaction channel in a dialogue-heavy courtroom sequence.
findings.suggestedFix: Thornton checked the room over the tops of his papers more than once.

findings.line: Thornton did not look up from the bench.
findings.reason: This is a weak procedural look beat after the chapter has already exceeded the eye/gaze cap.
findings.suggestedFix: Thornton stayed bent over the bench.

findings.line: Sylvanas looked at him.
findings.reason: This is an interchangeable connection beat and one of the weakest eye-led reactions in the Lor'themar exchange.
findings.suggestedFix: Sylvanas faced him.

findings.line: Then he looked at Sylvanas one last time.
findings.reason: The chapter has already exhausted its eye/gaze budget by this point, and this farewell beat is the most replaceable instance left.
findings.suggestedFix: Then he turned back to Sylvanas one last time.

summary.verdictReason: Fail: pass A found 22 mild KILLs across all three guides (5 negation pivots, 10 agency-laundering sentences, and 7 excess eye/gaze tics), which is far above the pass ceiling of 3 mild-only findings.
